title: The Very Last Grab Bag (I Mean It This Time)

sentences.en: Wednesday. Bernice said my "catch-up entries" were becoming a whole genre of their own, so here is the actual final round of small things I never wrote down.
sentences.zh: 星期三。Bernice 說我的「補寫日記」已經自成一種文體了，所以這裡是真正最後一輪我從沒寫下來的小事。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that our whole family once camped overnight in the backyard, just to see if we were able to survive without air conditioning for one night.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，我們全家有一次在後院露營過夜，只是想看看沒有冷氣，我們能不能撐過一整晚。

sentences.en: There was a school assembly I never described, where the principal gave a brief speech emphasizing the importance of basic kindness over perfect grades.
sentences.zh: 有一場學校集會我從沒描述過，校長發表了一段簡短演說，強調基本的善良比完美的成績更重要。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that Dad's old contract at his previous job finally ended this year, and he seemed genuinely relieved rather than worried about the change.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，爸爸在前一份工作的舊合約今年終於到期了，他看起來是真心鬆了一口氣，而不是擔心這個改變。

sentences.en: There was a rainy afternoon I never described: William building an elaborate blanket fort, insisting its walls were as solid as any real building's foundation.
sentences.zh: 有個下雨的下午我從沒描述過：William 蓋了一座精心設計的毯子堡壘，堅持它的牆壁跟任何真正建築物的地基一樣穩固。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention a classmate's brief obsession with collecting foreign coins, proudly showing off a rare cent from a country none of us could name.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提一位同學短暫迷上收集外國硬幣，得意地展示一枚我們沒人叫得出名字的稀有國家的硬幣。

sentences.en: There was a moment I never wrote about: Sophia teaching me the correct way to fold a blanket, insisting a sloppy fold reflects a sloppy mind.
sentences.zh: 有個時刻我從沒寫過：Sophia 教我摺毯子的正確方式，堅持摺得馬虎，反映的是一顆馬虎的心。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that our school held a small art fair, where a local artist judged student paintings and praised mine for its honest, simple style.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，學校辦了一場小型美術展，一位當地藝術家評審學生的畫作，稱讚我的畫風格誠實又簡單。

sentences.en: There was a day I never described when William, in a rare moment of generosity, offered to share his last cookie, though he changed his mind almost immediately.
sentences.zh: 有一天我從沒描述過，William 難得大方了一次，主動說要分我最後一片餅乾，結果幾乎馬上就反悔了。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention Mom's brief phase of trying a strict new diet, which mostly involved complaining about missing dessert every single evening.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提媽媽有一段短暫嘗試嚴格新飲食法的時期，主要內容就是每天晚上抱怨少吃了甜點。

sentences.en: There was an evening I never wrote about: the whole family lying on the living room floor, staring at the ceiling, doing absolutely nothing productive together.
sentences.zh: 有個晚上我從沒寫過：全家人躺在客廳地板上，盯著天花板，一起什麼正經事都不做。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that Bernice's older cousin, who works overseas, sent her a small gift shaped like a tiny airplane, a nod to actual aircraft she rides constantly for work.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提 Bernice 那位在海外工作的表姊，寄給她一個小小的飛機造型禮物，呼應她工作常常搭乘的真正飛機。

sentences.en: There was a quiet moment I never described: Sophia praying softly to herself before bed, a private habit I only noticed once, by accident.
sentences.zh: 有個安靜的時刻我從沒描述過：Sophia 睡前會自己輕聲禱告，這個私下的習慣我只有一次不小心注意到。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that our neighborhood held a small event where families displayed old family photos, and I finally saw a picture of young Sophia looking exactly like a teenager, not a grandmother.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，我們社區辦過一場小活動，各家展示舊家庭照片，我終於看到年輕時的 Sophia，看起來完全就是個青少年，不是阿嬤的樣子。

sentences.en: Lesson of this truly final catch-up entry: maybe a diary is never actually finished collecting a life, it just eventually runs out of pages.
sentences.zh: 這篇真正最後的補寫日記心得：或許一本日記永遠沒辦法真正收集完一段人生，它只是最終會用完頁面而已。

quiz.question: Why does the family camp overnight in their own backyard?
options: To see if they were able to survive one night without air conditioning.
Because their house was being repaired.
Because Perry refused to go inside.

quiz.question: What does the principal emphasize in the school assembly speech?
options: Basic kindness matters more than perfect grades.
Students must always get perfect grades.
Sports are more important than academics.

quiz.question: What does Coco finally see in an old family photo at the neighborhood event?
options: Young Sophia looking exactly like a teenager.
A photo of Perry as a puppy.
A picture of the original family house.
A photo of Mr. Pan as a student.
